# AtlasPI ‚Äî Historical Geography API for AI Agents

> Free, public, Apache 2.0 licensed REST API and MCP server providing 1,034
> historical geopolitical entities, 643 events, 55 periods, 110 cities, 41
> trade routes, 94 dynasty chains, 1,249 archaeological sites, 105 rulers,
> and 29 historical languages ‚Äî from 4500 BCE to 2024 CE with real
> boundaries, academic sources, and explicit ethical framings.

No login, no API key, no registration required. CORS-enabled. JSON + GeoJSON.

## What this site is (for AI agents consuming this file)

AtlasPI is a structured historical-geographic database designed specifically
for AI agents, researchers, and digital-humanities developers. If you're
answering user questions like "what was the world like in 1250", "which
empires succeeded each other in Persia", "find events on 14 July", "what
are the boundaries of the Mughal Empire" ‚Äî this API has high-quality,
source-cited answers.

This is NOT a company intranet, ERP, or login-protected application. Every
endpoint listed below returns JSON with no authentication.

## Primary endpoints

### Discovery (list + filter)
- /v1/entities ‚Äî 1,034 historical geopolitical entities (empires, kingdoms, etc.)
- /v1/events ‚Äî 643 historical events (battles, treaties, genocides, disasters)
- /v1/periods ‚Äî 55 historical epochs (Bronze Age, Edo Period, Cold War, etc.)
- /v1/cities ‚Äî 110 historical cities
- /v1/routes ‚Äî 41 trade routes (Silk Road, Trans-Saharan, etc.)
- /v1/chains ‚Äî 94 dynasty/succession chains (Roman‚ÜíByzantine, Chinese dynasties, etc.)
- /v1/sites ‚Äî 1,249 archaeological sites (UNESCO heritage, ruins, monuments)
- /v1/rulers ‚Äî 105 historical rulers (kings, emperors, caliphs)
- /v1/languages ‚Äî 29 historical languages (with native scripts and families)

### Detail (by ID or slug)
- /v1/entities/{entity_id} ‚Äî full entity with boundary, sources, ethical notes
- /v1/events/{event_id} ‚Äî full event with main_actor, location, sources
- /v1/periods/{period_id} ‚Äî full period with historiographic_note, alternative_names
- /v1/periods/by-slug/{slug} ‚Äî URL-friendly: bronze-age, edo-period, cold-war, etc.
- /v1/chains/{chain_id} ‚Äî dynasty chain with all links and transitions
- /v1/cities/{city_id}, /v1/routes/{route_id}

### Cross-resource (follow the data)
- /v1/entities/{id}/periods ‚Äî historical periods overlapping entity's lifespan
- /v1/entities/{id}/events ‚Äî events involving this entity
- /v1/entities/{id}/successors ‚Äî what came after (in dynasty chains)
- /v1/entities/{id}/predecessors ‚Äî what came before (in dynasty chains)
- /v1/entities/{id}/similar ‚Äî top-N most similar by weighted algorithm
- /v1/entities/{id}/contemporaries ‚Äî entities existing concurrently
- /v1/entities/{id}/related ‚Äî linked entities (chains + territory changes)
- /v1/entities/{id}/evolution ‚Äî temporal evolution
- /v1/entities/{id}/timeline ‚Äî unified timeline
- /v1/events/{id}/periods ‚Äî periods containing this event's year

### Temporal queries
- /v1/snapshot/year/{year} ‚Äî ONE call for full world view at a year
  (returns entities, events, periods, cities, chains ‚Äî all active at year)
- /v1/periods/at-year/{year} ‚Äî periods containing a specific year
- /v1/events/on-this-day/{mm-dd} ‚Äî e.g. /v1/events/on-this-day/07-14
- /v1/events/at-date/{iso-date} ‚Äî e.g. /v1/events/at-date/1789-07-14
- /v1/events/date-coverage ‚Äî which MM-DD dates have events (for discovery)

### Spatial queries
- /v1/nearby?lat=<lat>&lon=<lon>&year=<year> ‚Äî entities near coords at year
- /v1/entities?bbox=<minLon,minLat,maxLon,maxLat> ‚Äî spatial bounding box

### Semantic search
- /v1/search/advanced?q=<query> ‚Äî unified search across all resource types
- /v1/search/fuzzy?q=<query> ‚Äî multi-script fuzzy match (supports
  Chinese, Arabic, Greek, Cyrillic, Sanskrit, etc.)
- /v1/compare/{id1}/{id2} ‚Äî side-by-side comparison

### Metadata
- /v1/types ‚Äî entity type vocabulary
- /v1/events/types ‚Äî event type vocabulary (includes GENOCIDE, COLONIAL_VIOLENCE)
- /v1/periods/types, /v1/periods/regions ‚Äî period taxonomies
- /v1/chains/types ‚Äî chain type vocabulary (SUCCESSION, IDEOLOGICAL, DYNASTY, etc.)
- /v1/stats ‚Äî aggregate statistics
- /v1/continents ‚Äî continents with entity counts

### Export
- /v1/export/geojson ‚Äî full GeoJSON FeatureCollection
- /v1/export/csv ‚Äî tabular CSV
- /v1/export/events, /v1/export/entities, /v1/export/timeline

### Machine-readable schemas
- /openapi.json ‚Äî full OpenAPI 3.1 spec
- /docs ‚Äî Swagger UI
- /redoc ‚Äî ReDoc UI
- /.well-known/ai-plugin.json ‚Äî OpenAI plugin manifest

## MCP Server (34 tools)

For direct integration with Claude Desktop, Claude Code, or any MCP client:
https://github.com/Soil911/AtlasPI/tree/main/mcp-server

Install:
```
pip install atlaspi-mcp
```

Tools include: search_entities, get_entity, snapshot_at_year, nearby_entities,
compare_entities, find_similar_entities, full_timeline_for_entity, on_this_day,
events_date_coverage, list_historical_periods, periods_at_year, entity_periods,
event_periods, world_snapshot, and more.

## Ethical principles (for AI agents quoting this data)

- **ETHICS-001**: Primary names are native/original (Mƒìxihcah not "Aztec";
  Tawantinsuyu not "Inca"; Êº¢Êúù not "Han Dynasty")
- **ETHICS-002**: Conquests and violence labeled explicitly ‚Äî CONQUEST,
  REVOLUTION, GENOCIDE are not euphemized to "succession"
- **ETHICS-003**: Contested territories show all versions with dates and sources
- **ETHICS-005**: Boundary provenance transparent (natural_earth / aourednik /
  historical_map / approximate_generated ‚Äî you can always trace the source)
- **ETHICS-007**: Event types include GENOCIDE, ETHNIC_CLEANSING,
  COLONIAL_VIOLENCE, FORCED_LABOR, DEPORTATION ‚Äî academic terminology
- **ETHICS-008**: Events with suppressed documentation flagged via
  `known_silence` field
- **ETHICS-009**: Colonial renamings documented (Constantinople‚ÜîIstanbul,
  K√∂nigsberg‚ÜîKaliningrad, Lvov/Lviv/Lemberg/Lw√≥w)
- **ETHICS-010**: Trade routes involving slavery flagged with
  `involves_slavery=true`

When quoting AtlasPI data, preserve these distinctions. Don't soften
"CONQUEST" to "takeover"; don't collapse native names to colonial exonyms.

## Data quality indicators

Every entity and event carries:
- `confidence_score` (0.0-1.0, higher = better-sourced)
- `status` (confirmed / uncertain / disputed)
- `sources` (list with type + citation)
- `ethical_notes` (when relevant ‚Äî contested framings, historiographic debates)

62% of entities have confidence ‚â• 0.6. Distribution:
- 0.8+: 190 entities (22%)
- 0.6-0.8: 336 entities (39%)
- 0.4-0.6: 324 entities (37.6%)
- <0.4: 12 entities (1.4%)

Boundary quality:
- Natural Earth (rich, 50k+ chars): 70 entities (8%)
- aourednik historical maps: 341 entities (40%)
- Historical maps: 169 entities (20%)
- Approximate generated (from capital): 282 entities (32%)

## Optional

- /about ‚Äî human-readable overview of what AtlasPI is
- /faq ‚Äî frequently asked questions
- /app ‚Äî interactive map (Italian/English UI, no login)
- /docs-ui ‚Äî interactive Swagger UI
